item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm hardly ever startled or scared by SCP anymore, but the exploration logs did manage to creep me out. The structure and concept here are like the typical Series I-style scip, but written with modern site standards. It's quaint, but has massive and terrifying connotations. +1 for me

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Author, your article has a "title" on the mainlist but it's not from you and from a different user. Are you fine with this change?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The exploration log was great and always like D class with personality.
I really like this entry and won't be surprised if this will be the winner

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's interesting for sure. I gave it +1, but I do feel like it could use a little more fleshing out. I'm not sure what exactly it needs, but I feel like it's something. I did enjoy it though, and it's not to say it's bad by any means.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You're correct to an extent.

+ Spoiler
- Spoiler

What I was going for is that SCP-4963-2 instances actually channel broadcasts/recordings from alternate realities in an attempt to warn people of the true monster— the one our Foundation has yet to recover. This is why the log you hear the -2 instance say is just talking to "SCP-4963" despite the fact that, in baseline reality, it's just a portal.

When the first instance was channeling that broadcast, it was trying to tell the D-Class that if the Foundation doesn't find what they're hiding from, then there's a large possibility that this will leak into ours as well.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
From what i understand of the medical file at the end, SCP 4963-2 can in some way "predict the future".
I really enjoyed reading this. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
An exploration log will nearly always tickle me right. Although ambitious, I also appreciate the format screw near the end.

+1

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
This was created for the Cliche-Con 2019 contest and falls into Category 1. Cliches implemented are:

  • A doorway of any shape to a different dimension/timeline/world.
  • (Technically) Spooky scary murder monster(s)!

The image is from the Visual Records website in the Spectral category. Link found here.

Thanks to MrWrong's 001 Proposal for the terminal login text!
